# Bein Hashmashos

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Bain Hashmashos RT 13 Point 24 Degrees

The beginning of bain hashmashos of Rabbeinu Tam, using a degree-based version of the 58.5-minutes-after-sunset calculation.

It is calculated when the sun is 13.24° below the western geometric horizon after sunset. This is based on the sun’s position 58.5 minutes after sunset in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/), which calculates to 13.24° below geometric zenith.

Yisrael Vehazmanim Vol. III page 1028, No. 50 says that a dip of slightly less than 13° should be used. The documentation notes 13.2456° as the proper dip, truncated to 13.24°, giving a time about 1.5 seconds earlier lechumra.

The difference between 13.24° and 13° is given as 1 minute 10 seconds in Jerusalem during the equinox, 1 minute 29 seconds in Jerusalem during the solstice, and 1 minute 56 seconds in New York during the solstice.

This zman may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosRT13Point24Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosRT13Point24Degrees`

## Bain Hashmashos RT 13 Point 5 Minutes Before 7 Point 083 Degrees

The beginning of bain hashmashos of Rabbeinu Tam in a calculation based on 13.5 minutes before shkiah calculated as 7.083°.

It is calculated as 13.5 minutes, which is 3/4 of an 18-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), before shkiah calculated as 7.083°.

This zman may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosRT13Point5MinutesBefore7Point083Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosRT13Point5MinutesBefore7Point083Degrees`

## Bain Hashmashos RT 2 Stars

The beginning of bain hashmashos of Rabbeinu Tam according to the opinion of the Divrei Yosef, as cited in Yisrael Vehazmanim.

It is calculated as 5/18, or 27.77%, of the time between alos, calculated as 19.8° before sunrise, and sunrise. That amount of time is added to sunset.

This zman may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosRT2Stars`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosRT2Stars`

## Bain Hashmashos RT 58 Point 5 Minutes

The beginning of bain hashmashos of Rabbeinu Tam, calculated as 58.5 minutes after sunset.

Bain hashmashos is treated as 3/4 of a [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement) before tzais, or 3 1/4 mil after sunset. With a mil calculated as 18 minutes, 3.25 × 18 equals 58.5 minutes after sunset.

This zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosRT58Point5Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosRT58Point5Minutes`

## Bain Hashmashos Yereim 13 Point 5 Minutes

The beginning of bain hashmashos, or twilight, according to the [Yereim (Rabbi Eliezer of Metz)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_ben_Samuel). According to the Yereim, bain hashmashos starts 3/4 of a mil before sunset, and tzais or nightfall starts at sunset.

It is calculated as 13.5 minutes, or 3/4 of an 18-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), before sunset.

This zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim13Point5Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim13Point5Minutes`

## Bain Hashmashos Yereim 16 Point 875 Minutes

The beginning of bain hashmashos, or twilight, according to the [Yereim (Rabbi Eliezer of Metz)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_ben_Samuel). According to the Yereim, bain hashmashos starts 3/4 of a mil before sunset, and tzais or nightfall starts at sunset.

It is calculated as 16.875 minutes, or 3/4 of a 22.5-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), before sunset.

This zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim16Point875Minutes`

## Bain Hashmashos Yereim 18 Minutes

The beginning of bain hashmashos, or twilight, according to the [Yereim (Rabbi Eliezer of Metz)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_ben_Samuel). According to the Yereim, bain hashmashos starts 3/4 of a mil before sunset, and tzais or nightfall starts at sunset.

It is calculated as 18 minutes, or 3/4 of a 24-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), before sunset.

This zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim18Minutes`

## Bain Hashmashos Yereim 2 Point 1 Degrees

The beginning of bain hashmashos according to the [Yereim (Rabbi Eliezer of Metz)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_ben_Samuel). According to the Yereim, bain hashmashos starts 3/4 of a mil before sunset, and tzais or nightfall starts at sunset.

It is calculated when the sun is 2.1° above the horizon [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/) in Yerushalayim, corresponding to 13.5 minutes, or 3/4 of an 18-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), before sunset.

This zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim2Point1Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim2Point1Degrees`

## Bain Hashmashos Yereim 2 Point 8 Degrees

The beginning of bain hashmashos, or twilight, according to the [Yereim (Rabbi Eliezer of Metz)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_ben_Samuel). According to the Yereim, bain hashmashos starts 3/4 of a mil before sunset, and tzais, or nightfall, starts at sunset.

Calculated when the sun is 2.8° above the horizon [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/), corresponding to 16.875 minutes, or 3/4 of an 18-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), before sunset.

This zman may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim2Point8Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim2Point8Degrees`

## Bain Hashmashos Yereim 3 Point 05 Degrees

The beginning of bain hashmashos, or twilight, according to the [Yereim (Rabbi Eliezer of Metz)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_ben_Samuel). According to the Yereim, bain hashmashos starts 3/4 of a mil before sunset, and tzais, or nightfall, starts at sunset.

Calculated when the sun is 3.05° above the horizon [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/), corresponding to 18 minutes, or 3/4 of a 24-minute [mil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biblical_and_Talmudic_units_of_measurement), before sunset.

A refraction value of 0.5166° is used, rather than the traditional 0.566°, as a lechumra of about 14 seconds. This is described as more in line with actual refraction in Eretz Yisrael and is cited from [Rabbi Yedidya Manet](http://beinenu.com/rabbis/%D7%94%D7%A8%D7%91-%D7%99%D7%93%D7%99%D7%93%D7%99%D7%94-%D7%9E%D7%A0%D7%AA) in [Zmanei HaHalacha Lema'aseh](https://www.nli.org.il/en/books/NNL_ALEPH002542826/NLI).

The same 0.5166° refraction is also used by the [Luach Itim Lebinah](https://zmanim.online/).

More details are available in [The Yereim's Bain Hashmashos](https://kosherjava.com/2020/12/07/the-yereims-bein-hashmashos/).

This zman may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim3Point05Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getBainHashmashosYereim3Point05Degrees`
